item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that's fair. Looking back at this, there are a lot of holes here that don't appeal to my personal standards, which I'm considering fixing in a potential rewrite — I'll see what I can do. Thanks for the comment!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Echoing roget’s sentiments. This could’ve gone a lot further with the concept, and have been written a lot funnier. The decommisioning element feels shoehorned in and distracts from what should be an inherently entertaining anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, one of those is a horrible monster, and the other is the Dissector

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This started off well enough (the sticks detail was… strange in a fun way), but by the end there was really just… way, too much text for the story you're telling. This is especially a problem in the last 3 logs, which were basically "We're going to try this thing." <Thing works exactly as planned.>, no interesting or unexpected results. When your story runs out, don't make me keep reading.

For an example of what to do different, you don't need to have him making a speech about how much effort he put into the video. Just describe an overwrought video. That would give you an opportunity to put in more of the fun otherworld-ness.

(Too much text was an issue even before that. I don't need lengthy "realistic" decommissioning proposal documents. The point of "realistic" documentation is verisimilitude, and after that has been achieved it rapidly wears out its welcome.)

(Personal bias, but I guess it also kinda hurts that the description of the copyright scene really accurately gave me the impression of… let's say a character I'm unlikely to empathize with. That's just a minor point, though.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In universe, I'm not sure that would work, actually. Think about if you were an aspiring video producer and film critic, and some randos online ask you to shut down your channel. Would you do it? I'd guess probably not, and certainly not soon enough for the Foundation. The Foundation could reveal themselves as the powerful organization that they are to convince the being, but keep in mind that they don't know enough about this dimension to figure out whether that would come to bite them back in the ass; hence, the aggression.

Also, out-of-universe, that's a lot less fun to write, but eh.

Anyway, thank you for the read, and glad you enjoyed it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: honestly really liked this one, although i kind of would have preferred it if the foundation just nicely asked the being to stop putting videos on human machines

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
they made the nostalgia critic an SCP

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a concept I feel like was almost tailor-made for me to enjoy, I grew up on this exact sort of caustic critic content. But unfortunately, the execution here isn't doing it for me. The logs all kind of blend together in terms of what they're communicating, they're not building up to one final crescendo and then the ending is wholly unsatisfying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, in an earlier draft, the Dissector was a lot more caustic, leaning a bit more into the "why did they force anglerbeings in this movie!" terrain. Before I got anyone to crit this, though, I scrapped that, as I felt that would make them a little too caustic. After all, I don't want to make the Dissector an entirely unlikable douchebag.

Anyways, thank you very much for the comment, I'm glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-4659 or "The One Where the Foundation Drives the Angry Alien Filmography Nerd" off the webscape"

I like this, especially the part where the Foundation pretends to be fans and build up Dissector with constructive critiques… only to tear Dissector down like an atom bomb to a hedge bush with a wave of complaints. Though I have to admit: I feel the Dissector is not caustic enough, way too tame compared to the Internet reviewers I have seen. But maybe that is due to the medium of writing, which is not exactly that great for conveying these caustic stuff

+1 anyway

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Huge thanks to everyone who critted this in IRC chat, including Calibri Bold, DrUnoriginal, Ihp, JakdragonX, and TSDTom. Thank you very much for helping me out with this!

Also, should I mention Calibri Bold again for creating the Decommissioning Department…? Eh, sure. Thanks to you too!

more from barredowl
